threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait at the platform for a Tube that’ll never come, not knowing strikes are the norm. Welcome to Hackney’s charm!
Caught the 35 last night, and a guy was passionately arguing with a lamppost about Brexit. Lamppost stayed silent, unsurprisingly.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I caught a burlesque show in an old cinema, and the crowd cheered louder for the cats on stage than the performers.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop open at 4 AM serving currywurst and freshly squeezed orange juice. It’s a late-night ritual for many.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows that the bodega on 24th has the best pan dulce, but you won’t find it on Yelp.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading passengers on the 21 bus yesterday. Gave a full opera performance just as we hit a pothole. Classic Muni moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia's plazas are just for photos, but locals use them to catch up, enjoying the daily life that’s far from picture-perfect.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look confused at the Bicing stations, trying to figure out how to unlock the bikes while everyone else pedals past.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road at pedestrian lights instead of just weaving through traffic like a local. Those buttons are just for show.
Caught a late 59 last weekend; a bloke in a top hat serenaded the driver with a sax. You never know what you'll hear at 2 am in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy in a suit bartering with a street vendor over a kebab while his dog wore a tiny bowtie. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a techno rave in an abandoned factory on a Tuesday night—where the dress code is sheer creativity and the after-party is at the Kiezkneipe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their disdain for tourists who still think Dolores Park is just for picnics. It's a social battlefield now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus with a full-on opera performance in the back. Only in SF will you find a stage on wheels. Classic Muni moment!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume every corner café serves churros. Most locals prefer a simple pa amb tomàquet and a café amb llet, no frills.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their reluctance to sit at a bar table when there are empty seats; they still expect to wait for a 'proper' table.
